articles.title: Who Really Stopped Topuria vs Gaethje?
articles.description: The dust has settled on the White House card, and Chael Sonnen is revisiting two of the biggest talking points from the event. First, Chael reexamines the controversial stoppage in Ilia Topuria vs Justin Gaethje and explains why he now believes Topuria's brother may have been telling the referee the fight was over—not asking for one more round. Then Chael turns his attention to the growing debate surrounding Ciryl Gane's elbows against Alex Pereira. Were the strikes legal? If they weren't, should the result be reviewed? articles.title: The Night Ilia Became a Legend #ufc
articles.description: Sometimes a fighter loses the fight but proves something even bigger. According to Chael Sonnen, that's exactly what happened with Ilia Topuria. In this breakdown, Chael explains why Topuria's performance against Justin Gaethje may have elevated his reputation despite the result. He points to moments that showcased every aspect of Ilia's game, from elite body punching to high-level grappling transitions. Chael highlights a sequence where Topuria repeatedly attacked the body and actually dropped Gaethje with body shots—something almost unheard of at the championship level in the UFC lightweight division. Beyond the striking, Chael was equally impressed by Topuria's jiu-jitsu. Moving seamlessly from mount to armbar attempts, transitioning through knee-on-belly, and attacking with submissions, Ilia displayed the complete skill set that has made many believe he's one of the most talented fighters in the sport today. The biggest issue wasn't skill—it was endurance. After a lengthy layoff and major changes outside the cage, questions about conditioning followed Topuria into the fight. Yet even in defeat, Chael believes he showed superior technique, fight IQ, and athleticism. Now the lightweight division becomes even more intriguing. Gaethje sits atop the mountain, while names like Max Holloway and Charles Oliveira remain in the title picture. One thing is certain: regardless of the result, Chael believes both Topuria and Gaethje added to their legacies. For more UFC analysis and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #IliaTopuria #JustinGaethje #MaxHolloway #CharlesOliveira #ChaelSonnen #MMA #LightweightTitle

articles.title: Is Ilia Topuria REALLY That Good? #ufc
articles.description: Is Ilia Topuria really as good as the MMA world says he is? Chael Sonnen went directly to some of the sport's most respected voices to find out—and the answer wasn't close. In this breakdown, Chael reacts to comments from Jorge Masvidal, who compared Topuria's impact to Michael Jordan and suggested that if Ilia wins titles across multiple divisions, his place among the greatest fighters ever would become impossible to deny. That sparked a bigger question: have fans actually seen enough from Topuria to make those claims? Chael took that question to a room full of MMA veterans, including Chris Weidman, Dustin Poirier, Dean Thomas, and Michael Chandler. According to Chael, the response was unanimous: yes, Topuria is every bit as good as advertised. From his knockout victories over Alexander Volkanovski and Charles Oliveira to his championship success across divisions, Chael argues there is currently no obvious style or skill set that appears capable of exposing a weakness. In his view, the only thing preventing Topuria from holding multiple titles simultaneously has been UFC paperwork—not competition. articles.title: Who’s the Bigger Star: Ilia or Islam? #ufc
articles.description: Who is the bigger UFC star right now: Ilia Topuria or Islam Makhachev? Chael Sonnen believes the answer may be closer than fans think—and the UFC could eventually settle the debate for everyone. In this breakdown, Chael examines Topuria's ongoing pursuit of a superfight with Islam and the risks that come with it. If the UFC puts these two champions together, one of them has to lose. Chael questions whether that outcome makes sense from a business standpoint, especially when both fighters are among the promotion's biggest attractions. Chael also reacts to Topuria's bold comments about Makhachev. Ilia has publicly claimed that Islam can't wrestle with him and that he'll knock him out. Those are enormous statements against one of the most dominant champions in MMA. Chael points out that fans haven't seen much of Topuria's takedown defense—not because it's weak, but because opponents rarely attempt to wrestle him. The bigger question may be star power. Chael argues that if Islam and Ilia ever headline the same card against different opponents, the UFC will instantly reveal who the bigger draw is by who gets the main event slot. Until then, one thing is clear: Topuria isn't waiting his turn. He's calling his shots, demanding opponents, and forcing the sport to react.
